description: Install, update, and manage the Winix CLI tool suite.

platform.value_on_unix: Manages all Winix tools with a single command across Windows and macOS

options.description: Structured JSON metadata for AI agents
Show commands that would be run without executing them
Write into committed project files (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) instead of user/global agent config (agents only)
Force the Claude home/file even when absent: user scope → ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md; --project → include CLAUDE.md (agents only)
Force the Codex user home (~/.codex/AGENTS.md) even when absent (agents user scope only)
Package manager to use: winget, scoop, brew, dotnet
Project directory for --project (agents only; default: current directory)
Coloured output: auto (default when omitted), always, or never

exit_codes.description: One or more tools failed; or (agents) the pointer block is absent or stale, or (user scope) no agent home exists
Internal error (manifest fetch/parse failure, or agents file I/O failure)

io.stdout: JSON metadata when --json is set, otherwise unused
io.stderr: Progress and result lines, one per tool.

examples.description: Install all Winix tools using the default package manager
List all tools and their install status
Show a summary of installed tools
Write the Winix pointer into your user agent config (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, ~/.codex/AGENTS.md)
Write a conditional pointer into this repo's AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md (for teams standardized on Winix)
Report whether your user agent config carries a current pointer (exit 1 if not)
Remove the Winix pointer block from your user agent config
